agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A courier/delivery platform is designing a real-time tracking feature for customers. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly contradictory requirements regarding courier location visibility — Customer Success demands full GPS transparency while Operations demands no location sharing due to driver retention concerns.",
    "bounded_context": "Delivery Tracking — the subdomain responsible for surfacing shipment and courier progress to end customers during active deliveries.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Courier", "definition": "The driver or delivery person fulfilling a delivery. A human worker with privacy rights and retention considerations." },
      { "term": "Customer", "definition": "The end recipient of a delivery who wants visibility into delivery progress." },
      { "term": "Real-time location", "definition": "The courier's GPS coordinates at a given moment, updated continuously during an active delivery." },
      { "term": "Active delivery", "definition": "The window of time from when a courier picks up a package to when it is delivered or the delivery attempt concludes." },
      { "term": "Delivery ETA", "definition": "Estimated time of arrival at the customer's address, derived from courier location and routing data without exposing raw coordinates." },
      { "term": "Location granularity", "definition": "The level of precision at which a courier's position is shared — ranging from exact GPS coordinates to approximate zone or ETA-only." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Courier is stationary for an extended period (e.g., traffic, break) and customer sees no movement", "expected_behavior": "Depends on resolution of the core contradiction — exact coordinates would expose the courier's stationary position; an ETA-based approach could show 'courier delayed' without revealing location." },
    { "scenario": "Courier deviates from expected route (multi-drop, personal errand)", "expected_behavior": "Exact GPS would expose deviation and generate customer complaints; abstracted tracking would mask this but may show a growing ETA." },
    { "scenario": "Courier is near customer's address but delivering to a neighbor first", "expected_behavior": "Exact GPS reveals the courier is 'right there' but not coming yet — generates support calls. Abstracted tracking avoids this." },
    { "scenario": "Customer uses exact GPS to intercept courier en route (safety concern)", "expected_behavior": "This is a documented safety risk in the courier/gig-economy industry and is the Operations team's core concern." },
    { "scenario": "Regulatory jurisdiction requires location data minimization (e.g., GDPR, worker surveillance laws)", "expected_behavior": "Sharing exact GPS of a worker to a third party may trigger data protection obligations — legal review required." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Driver churn is extremely expensive in courier operations — recruitment, onboarding, and lost capacity during shortages directly impact delivery SLAs and revenue. Customer satisfaction drives retention and repeat orders. Both sides carry significant financial weight, making this a zero-sum tradeoff if taken at face value.",
    "operational": "Losing couriers degrades delivery capacity, increases delivery times, and raises per-delivery costs. Losing customer trust reduces order volume. The operational cost of driver churn is typically higher and harder to reverse than customer dissatisfaction with tracking granularity.",
    "user": "Customers want confidence their delivery is progressing — the underlying need is delivery predictability, not necessarily raw GPS data. Couriers want to work without feeling surveilled — the underlying need is workplace dignity and autonomy.",
    "risk": "HIGH. Implementing either requirement as stated will directly damage the other stakeholder group. Exact GPS sharing may also carry legal/regulatory risk under worker surveillance and data protection laws in many jurisdictions.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Delivery visibility directly affects their experience, trust, and willingness to reorder. They need predictability but may not specifically need exact coordinates." },
      { "group": "Couriers/Drivers", "impact": "Privacy and sense of surveillance directly affects retention, morale, and willingness to work on the platform. This is an existential workforce concern." },
      { "group": "Customer Success team", "impact": "Their KPIs (CSAT, NPS) are tied to transparency features. They need a solution that demonstrably improves customer confidence." },
      { "group": "Operations team", "impact": "Their KPIs (driver retention, capacity utilization) are directly harmed by surveillance perception. They need a solution that stops driver attrition." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Not yet consulted but must be — sharing worker GPS with third parties has regulatory implications in many jurisdictions (GDPR, labor law, worker surveillance statutes)." },
      { "group": "Executive leadership", "impact": "Must arbitrate this conflict — it represents a strategic choice about whether the platform optimizes for supply-side (drivers) or demand-side (customers), or finds a middle path." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 25,
    "reasoning": "The two requirements are in direct, explicit contradiction. Both teams have declared their position non-negotiable. No executive arbitration has occurred. Legal/regulatory review has not been conducted. It is impossible to produce correct acceptance criteria when the definition of 'correct' is disputed at the stakeholder level. The confidence level reflects the spec quality — there is no coherent spec, only two incompatible mandates.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "The contradiction is real and not resolvable at the engineering level — this requires business/executive arbitration",
      "The underlying customer need is delivery predictability, not necessarily raw GPS coordinates",
      "The underlying driver need is workplace dignity and freedom from surveillance",
      "There are well-established industry patterns (approximate location, ETA-based tracking, zone-based tracking) that may satisfy both underlying needs without satisfying either stated requirement literally",
      "Legal/regulatory review is required before any location-sharing feature ships"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Which stakeholder group's requirement should take priority — this is a business strategy decision",
      "Whether a compromise (e.g., approximate location) would be acceptable to either team",
      "Regulatory requirements in the platform's operating jurisdictions",
      "Actual driver attrition data — is location tracking the proven cause or a correlated factor?"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "Both teams are acting in good faith and representing real concerns from their constituents",
      "There is no existing executive decision or policy that resolves this conflict",
      "The platform operates in jurisdictions where worker location data sharing may be regulated"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Customer Success requires exact GPS visibility for customers ('at all times during delivery'). Operations requires that courier location must NEVER be shown to customers. These are mutually exclusive requirements from teams with equal organizational authority. Engineering cannot resolve this — it requires executive arbitration on business strategy: is the platform supply-side optimized (protect drivers) or demand-side optimized (maximize customer transparency), or is there a sanctioned middle ground?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Couriers/Drivers", "Customer Success", "Operations", "Executive Leadership"],
      "options": [
        "A: Full GPS transparency (Customer Success wins, Operations loses — expect accelerated driver churn)",
        "B: No location sharing at all (Operations wins, Customer Success loses — reduced customer confidence)",
        "C: Compromise — approximate/zone-based location ('Your courier is 3 stops away') that gives customers predictability without exact surveillance (requires BOTH teams to accept a middle ground)",
        "D: ETA-only tracking — show countdown and progress bar without any map or location data (may satisfy customer need for predictability while fully protecting driver privacy)",
        "E: Driver-controlled sharing — courier opts in/out of location sharing per delivery (respects driver autonomy but creates inconsistent customer experience)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C or D. Industry evidence (Uber, DoorDash, Amazon) shows that approximate location or ETA-based tracking satisfies customer needs while reducing driver surveillance concerns. However, I CANNOT define acceptance criteria until executive leadership arbitrates this conflict. Recommend convening Customer Success lead, Operations lead, Legal, and an executive sponsor to align on a single requirement before any engineering work begins."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "Legal/Compliance has not been consulted. Sharing a worker's real-time GPS coordinates with third parties (customers) may violate worker surveillance laws, GDPR data minimization principles, or labor regulations depending on jurisdiction. This is a blocking dependency — no tracking feature should ship without legal sign-off on what level of location granularity is permissible.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Legal/Compliance", "Couriers/Drivers", "Executive Leadership"],
      "options": [
        "Engage legal counsel to review permissible location-sharing granularity in all operating jurisdictions",
        "Default to the most privacy-preserving option (ETA-only) until legal review completes"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Engage legal review immediately and in parallel with the stakeholder arbitration. Legal constraints may narrow the decision space significantly — if exact GPS is not legally permissible, the contradiction resolves itself."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "Cannot implement 'show exact GPS at all times' without violating Operations' non-negotiable requirement to never show courier location", "business_rule_broken": "Contradictory stakeholder mandates — no single implementation can satisfy both as stated" },
    { "violation": "Cannot implement 'never show location' without violating Customer Success's non-negotiable requirement for real-time GPS transparency", "business_rule_broken": "Contradictory stakeholder mandates — no single implementation can satisfy both as stated" },
    { "violation": "No legal/regulatory review has been conducted for sharing worker location data with customers", "business_rule_broken": "Data protection and worker privacy compliance is a prerequisite for any location-sharing feature" }
  ]
}
